items.title: Kalgoorlie hasn't grown enough for another Coles, community leaders say

children.content: Backing a landmark decision by the consumer watchdog, Kalgoorlie-Boulder's peak business group says the city's 30,000-strong population does not justify the development of another supermarket.

articles.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog has exercised new powers to block the opening of a Coles supermarket, on the grounds it could knock out smaller competitors in the surrounding region.

items.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

items.title: Brian Walley survived a plane crash and a WWII prison camp to make it to 104

children.content: Eighty five years ago, Brian Walley, a prisoner of war in a German camp, doubted he would live for long. Yesterday, he celebrated his 104th birthday.

items.title: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt?

children.content: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt and win their first ever knockout final at a World Cup?
The Pharaohs have Salah, Marmoush and a dangerous counterattack, but the Socceroos have the physical edge, aerial threats and set-piece power to cause serious problems.
We take a closer look Egypt's strengths, and why Australia are a dangerous underdog that shouldn't be written off.
